3.happening: Create an empty array for the cleaned characters.
3.why: This is the O(n) extra memory the in-place version avoids.
3.invariant: filtered will hold only letters and digits, lowercased.

4.happening: Look at each character of the input in turn.
4.why: We decide which characters to keep.
4.invariant: filtered holds the kept characters so far.

5.happening: Is this character ({ch}) a letter or digit?
5.why: Punctuation, spaces, and symbols are dropped.

6.happening: Keep it — append {ch} (lowercased) to the clean copy.
6.why: Lowercasing now makes the later comparison case-insensitive for free.
6.invariant: filtered grows by one cleaned character.

7.happening: Point left at the start of the cleaned array.
7.why: One end of the palindrome check.

8.happening: Point right at the end of the cleaned array.
8.why: The other end of the check.
8.invariant: right begins at the last index.

9.happening: While the pointers haven't met, keep comparing.
9.why: Once they cross, every pair has matched.
9.invariant: left < right means characters remain to check.

10.why: The copy is already clean and lowercase, so a direct compare suffices.
10.invariant: Matching ends keep the palindrome possible.

11.happening: The ends differ — not a palindrome.

12.happening: They matched — move the left pointer inward.
12.why: This pair is confirmed; check the next in.
12.invariant: left advances past a matched character.

13.why: Close in on the middle from both sides.
13.invariant: right retreats past a matched character.

14.happening: All pairs matched — it's a palindrome.
14.why: Correct, but it required a full cleaned copy (O(n) space).

init.why: We build the answer in a second array first.

loop.happening: Iterating — first to build the copy, then to compare it.

check.happening: Deciding what to keep, or comparing the two ends.
check.why: Keep only alphanumerics; then compare symmetric pairs.

update.happening: Appending a cleaned character or setting up the pointers.
update.why: The clean copy grows, then the two-pointer check begins.

move.why: A matched pair lets us close in on the middle.

return.happening: All pairs matched — it's a palindrome.
return.why: Correct, but it used O(n) extra memory.
